visa_types.name: Tousrim
visa_types.documents_required:
Schengen Visa Application Requirements (Lithuania)
- Application Form (https://www.migracija.lt/): Completed Schengen visa application form through
- Photograph: One recent photo, glued to the form (not stapled).
- Travel Documents:
- Flight reservations showing the applicant’s name.
- Accommodation reservations with the applicant’s name.
- Insurance: Proof of travel insurance covering the entire stay in the Schengen area.
- For student: school registration.
- For employees: certificate of employment stating recruitment date, position, and salary.
- For self employed: company registration and tax card.
- Bank statement for the last 6 months. For minors, a parent’s bank statement.
- First time Applicants: Shahadat at Taharrukat translated into English.
- Language of Documents: All documents must be in English or Lithuanian, or accompanied by an official translation. Documents originally issued in English do not require translation.
- For Applicants Under 18:
- Original birth certificate translated into English.
- For Minors travelling alone: notarized certificate confirming the consent of both parents or legal guardian(s) to the minor’s travel. Copies of the parents’ or legal guardian(s)’ passports.
- For Minors travelling with one parent or legal guardian: notarized certificate confirming the consent of the non-accompanying parent or legal guardian to the minor’s travel. Copy of the parent or legal guardian’s passport . or legal guardian shall be signed in front of VFSGlobal agent,
- in case of one guardian is residing in another country, he/she should visit the nearest Lithuanian /Egyptian Embassy and sign the consent.
Travel Rules for Lithuania
- If Lithuania is not the sole destination, the following conditions must be met:
- Applicant must spend more days in Lithuania than in any other Schengen country.
- Applicant spends an equal number of days in Lithuania and another country, Lithuania must be the first destination.
- If these conditions are not satisfied, the application will not be accepted.
- Applicants visiting multiple countries must provide a detailed travel itinerary.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees applicable for Tourist, Relatives/Friend, Business, Sports and Cultural are: -

[Table:    Schengen Visa]
All optional services are non-refundable.

Applicants exempted from visa fee:
- Family members of citizens of Lithuania, other European Union member states and European Free Trade Association member states.
- In cases provided for in the EU Visa Facilitation Agreements.
- Other cases provided for in Article 16 of the Visa Code.
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Egyptian pounds is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
The Visa fee & service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
visa_types.processing_time:
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa varies accordingly to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application is given. The regular visa processing time is 15 days upon completion of the application, the processing time may be extended to 30 or even 60 days calendar days, in cases that require a detailed assessment.
Applicant’s lack of travel plan will not fall on Embassy / Representative Office priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.forms:
For *online Visa Application *Form of the Republic of Lithuania which is designed to complete and submit the Application for a Schengen visa to the consular services of the Republic of Lithuania.
Technical specifications 
If you want to use Migris on your computer, here's what it takes:
- Microsoft Windows,
- Firefox 18.0, Internet Explorer IE 9, Google Chrome 24.0 or more recent versions,
- Adobe Acrobat Reader 10 (to download the Application Form),
- a printer (to print the Application Form).
To fill your application form kindly click here (https://www.migracija.lt/)
Checklists:
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a tourist visa.
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a business visa.
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a sport visa.
- Please
 
click here  (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/lithuania/egypt/english/pdf/Lithuania-checklist-Visiting-family.pdf)
to download the checklist form for a visiting family visa.
visa_types.overview:
Overview

If you are planning a short visit to Lithuania for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Lithuania is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa for a stay of up to 90 days in any period of 180 days. Please note that the following essentials must be met by all applicants submitting their applications for visa.
- Passport should have at least two blank pages.
- Passport should be valid for at least 3 months from the date on which the applicant intends to leave the SCHENGEN territory, or, in the case of multiple journeys the date on which the applicant intends to leave it for the last time
- Applications are only accepted within 6 months of the requested date of arrival
- Relevant Forms & Photograph: Fully completed application form appropriate to the type of visa.
- Application will be accepted only until 20 days (excluding Visa Application Center handling time) before the date of travel.
The collection of passports at the Visa Application Center can be done by:
- The applicant himself /herself.
- A representative carrying a photo id of himself/herself and an authority letter from the applicant.
- The applicant can request Courier services on the submission day.
- IMPORTANT
- Applications can be lodged earliest Six (6) months before the planned trip.
- Visas are not issued on the day of the application and may take up to 4 weeks, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation where the authorities of the represented Member State are consulted. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period for the decision may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
- Holders of Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control at the point of entry and are not guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

If you are planning a short visit to Lithuania for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Lithuania is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa for a stay of up to 90 days in any period of 180 days. Please note that the following essentials must be met by all applicants submitting their applications for visa.
- Passport should have at least two blank pages.
- Passport should be valid for at least 3 months from the date on which the applicant intends to leave the SCHENGEN territory, or, in the case of multiple journeys the date on which the applicant intends to leave it for the last time
- Applications are only accepted within 6 months of the requested date of arrival
- Relevant Forms & Photograph: Fully completed application form appropriate to the type of visa.
- Application will be accepted only until 20 days (excluding Visa Application Center handling time) before the date of travel.
The collection of passports at the Visa Application Center can be done by:
- The applicant himself /herself.
- A representative carrying a photo id of himself/herself and an authority letter from the applicant.
- The applicant can request Courier services on the submission day.
- IMPORTANT
- Applications can be lodged earliest Six (6) months before the planned trip.
- Visas are not issued on the day of the application and may take up to 4 weeks, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation where the authorities of the represented Member State are consulted. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period for the decision may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
- Holders of Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control at the point of entry and are not guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for Tourist, Relatives/Friend, Business, Sports and Cultural are: -

[Table:    Schengen Visa]
All optional services are non-refundable.

Applicants exempted from visa fee:
- Family members of citizens of Lithuania, other European Union member states and European Free Trade Association member states.
- In cases provided for in the EU Visa Facilitation Agreements.
- Other cases provided for in Article 16 of the Visa Code.
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Egyptian pounds is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
The Visa fee & service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Schengen Visa Application Requirements (Lithuania)
- Application Form (https://www.migracija.lt/): Completed Schengen visa application form through
- Photograph: One recent photo, glued to the form (not stapled).
- Travel Documents:
- Flight reservations showing the applicant’s name.
- Accommodation reservations with the applicant’s name.
- Insurance: Proof of travel insurance covering the entire stay in the Schengen area.
- For student: school registration.
- For employees: certificate of employment stating recruitment date, position, and salary.
- For self employed: company registration and tax card.
- Bank statement for the last 6 months. For minors, a parent’s bank statement.
- First time Applicants: Shahadat at Taharrukat translated into English.
- Language of Documents: All documents must be in English or Lithuanian, or accompanied by an official translation. Documents originally issued in English do not require translation.
- For Applicants Under 18:
- Original birth certificate translated into English.
- For Minors travelling alone: notarized certificate confirming the consent of both parents or legal guardian(s) to the minor’s travel. Copies of the parents’ or legal guardian(s)’ passports.
- For Minors travelling with one parent or legal guardian: notarized certificate confirming the consent of the non-accompanying parent or legal guardian to the minor’s travel. Copy of the parent or legal guardian’s passport . or legal guardian shall be signed in front of VFSGlobal agent,
- in case of one guardian is residing in another country, he/she should visit the nearest Lithuanian /Egyptian Embassy and sign the consent.
Travel Rules for Lithuania
- If Lithuania is not the sole destination, the following conditions must be met:
- Applicant must spend more days in Lithuania than in any other Schengen country.
- Applicant spends an equal number of days in Lithuania and another country, Lithuania must be the first destination.
- If these conditions are not satisfied, the application will not be accepted.
- Applicants visiting multiple countries must provide a detailed travel itinerary.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in color and:
- Size of Photo is 5mm X 4.5mm
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling 90% of the photo is the head of applicant (without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa varies accordingly to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application is given. The regular visa processing time is 15 days upon completion of the application, the processing time may be extended to 30 or even 60 days calendar days, in cases that require a detailed assessment.
Applicant’s lack of travel plan will not fall on Embassy / Representative Office priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

## 

## Download Forms
For *online Visa Application *Form of the Republic of Lithuania which is designed to complete and submit the Application for a Schengen visa to the consular services of the Republic of Lithuania.
Technical specifications 
If you want to use Migris on your computer, here's what it takes:
- Microsoft Windows,
- Firefox 18.0, Internet Explorer IE 9, Google Chrome 24.0 or more recent versions,
- Adobe Acrobat Reader 10 (to download the Application Form),
- a printer (to print the Application Form).
To fill your application form kindly click here (https://www.migracija.lt/)
Checklists:
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a tourist visa.
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a business visa.
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a sport visa.
- Please
 
click here  (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/lithuania/egypt/english/pdf/Lithuania-checklist-Visiting-family.pdf)
to download the checklist form for a visiting family visa.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Schengen Visa Application Requirements (Lithuania)
- Application Form (https://www.migracija.lt/): Completed Schengen visa application form through
- Photograph: One recent photo, glued to the form (not stapled).
- Travel Documents:
- Flight reservations showing the applicant’s name.
- Accommodation reservations with the applicant’s name.
- Insurance: Proof of travel insurance covering the entire stay in the Schengen area.
- For student: school registration.
- For employees: certificate of employment stating recruitment date, position, and salary.
- For self employed: company registration and tax card.
- Bank statement for the last 6 months. For minors, a parent’s bank statement.
- First time Applicants: Shahadat at Taharrukat translated into English.
- Language of Documents: All documents must be in English or Lithuanian, or accompanied by an official translation. Documents originally issued in English do not require translation.
- For Applicants Under 18:
- Original birth certificate translated into English.
- For Minors travelling alone: notarized certificate confirming the consent of both parents or legal guardian(s) to the minor’s travel. Copies of the parents’ or legal guardian(s)’ passports.
- For Minors travelling with one parent or legal guardian: notarized certificate confirming the consent of the non-accompanying parent or legal guardian to the minor’s travel. Copy of the parent or legal guardian’s passport . or legal guardian shall be signed in front of VFSGlobal agent,
- in case of one guardian is residing in another country, he/she should visit the nearest Lithuanian /Egyptian Embassy and sign the consent.

Documents Required for Business Purposes:
- Official Invitation from the Inviting Company
Must be issued on company letterhead, stamped, and signed, and should include:
- Full address and contact details of the company
- Nature of the business
- Name and position of the authorized signatory
- Purpose and duration of the visit
- Details of who will cover travel and living expenses
- Confirmation of whether the sponsor provides a financial guarantee for the applicant’s return to Egypt
- Validated Invitation Letter

An invitation letter from the inviting company must be certified by the competent local authorities.
Note: Certain Member States (Austria, Finland, Hungary, Italy, Latvia, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, and Sweden) require a specific form for the invitation letter. Please consult the website of the relevant Member State for further details.
Travel Rules for Lithuania
- If Lithuania is not the sole destination, the following conditions must be met:
- Applicant must spend more days in Lithuania than in any other Schengen country.
- Applicant spends an equal number of days in Lithuania and another country, Lithuania must be the first destination.
- If these conditions are not satisfied, the application will not be accepted.
- Applicants visiting multiple countries must provide a detailed travel itinerary.

The Visa fee & service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

visa_types.name: Visiting Family /Friends
visa_types.documents_required:
Schengen Visa Application Requirements (Lithuania)
- Application Form (https://www.migracija.lt/)
: Completed Schengen visa application form through
- Photograph: One recent photo, glued to the form (not stapled).
- Travel Documents:
- Flight reservations showing the applicant’s name.
- Accommodation reservations with the applicant’s name.
- Insurance: Proof of travel insurance covering the entire stay in the Schengen area.
- For students: school registration.
- For employees: certificate of employment stating recruitment date, position, and salary.
- For self employed: company registration and tax card.
- Bank statement for the last 6 months. For minors, a parent’s bank statement.
- First time Applicants: Shahadat at Taharrukat translated into English.
- Language of Documents: All documents must be in English or Lithuanian, or accompanied by an official translation. Documents originally issued in English do not require translation.
- For Applicants Under 18:
- Original birth certificate translated into English.
- For Minors travelling alone: notarized certificate confirming the consent of both parents or legal guardian(s) to the minor’s travel. Copies of the parents’ or legal guardian(s)’ passports.
- For Minors travelling with one parent or legal guardian: notarized certificate confirming the consent of the non-accompanying parent or legal guardian to the minor’s travel. Copy of the parent or legal guardian’s passport . or legal guardian shall be signed in front of VFS Global agent,
- in case of one guardian is residing in another country, he/she should visit the nearest Lithuanian /Egyptian Embassy and sign the consent.
- Invitation letter:
- An invitation letter signed by host family/friends.
- The following Member States' consulates require that a specific form be used for the invitation letter: Austria, Finland, France, Germany, Hungary, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Slovenia, Slovakia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.

- Note: Movement certificate is not required for Visiting Family and Friends.
- If Lithuania is not the sole destination, the following conditions must be met:
- Applicant must spend more days in Lithuania than in any other Schengen country.
- Applicant spends an equal number of days in Lithuania and another country, Lithuania must be the first destination.
- If these conditions are not satisfied, the application will not be accepted.
- Applicants visiting multiple countries must provide a detailed travel itinerary.

visa_types.overview:
Overview

If you are planning a short visit to Lithuania for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Lithuania is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa for a stay of up to 90 days in any period of 180 days. Please note that the following essentials must be met by all applicants submitting their applications for visa.
- Passport should have at least two blank pages.
- Passport should be valid for at least 3 months from the date on which the applicant intends to leave the SCHENGEN territory, or, in the case of multiple journeys the date on which the applicant intends to leave it for the last time
- Applications are only accepted within 6 months of the requested date of arrival
- Relevant Forms & Photograph: Fully completed application form appropriate to the type of visa.
- Application will be accepted only until 20 days (excluding Visa Application Center handling time) before the date of travel.
The collection of passports at the Visa Application Center can be done by:
- The applicant himself /herself.
- A representative carrying a photo id of himself/herself and an authority letter from the applicant.
- The applicant can request Courier services on the submission day.
- IMPORTANT
- Applications can be lodged earliest Six (6) months before the planned trip.
- Visas are not issued on the day of the application and may take up to 4 weeks, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation where the authorities of the represented Member State are consulted. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period for the decision may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
- Holders of Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control at the point of entry and are not guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

If you are planning a short visit to Lithuania for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Lithuania is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa for a stay of up to 90 days in any period of 180 days. Please note that the following essentials must be met by all applicants submitting their applications for visa.
- Passport should have at least two blank pages.
- Passport should be valid for at least 3 months from the date on which the applicant intends to leave the SCHENGEN territory, or, in the case of multiple journeys the date on which the applicant intends to leave it for the last time
- Applications are only accepted within 6 months of the requested date of arrival
- Relevant Forms & Photograph: Fully completed application form appropriate to the type of visa.
- Application will be accepted only until 20 days (excluding Visa Application Center handling time) before the date of travel.
The collection of passports at the Visa Application Center can be done by:
- The applicant himself /herself.
- A representative carrying a photo id of himself/herself and an authority letter from the applicant.
- The applicant can request Courier services on the submission day.
- IMPORTANT
- Applications can be lodged earliest Six (6) months before the planned trip.
- Visas are not issued on the day of the application and may take up to 4 weeks, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed or in cases of representation where the authorities of the represented Member State are consulted. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period for the decision may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
- Holders of Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control at the point of entry and are not guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for Tourist, Relatives/Friend, Business, Sports and Cultural are: -

[Table:    Schengen Visa]
All optional services are non-refundable.

Applicants exempted from visa fee:
- Family members of citizens of Lithuania, other European Union member states and European Free Trade Association member states.
- In cases provided for in the EU Visa Facilitation Agreements.
- Other cases provided for in Article 16 of the Visa Code.
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Egyptian pounds is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
The Visa fee & service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required

Schengen Visa Application Requirements (Lithuania)
- Application Form (https://www.migracija.lt/)
: Completed Schengen visa application form through
- Photograph: One recent photo, glued to the form (not stapled).
- Travel Documents:
- Flight reservations showing the applicant’s name.
- Accommodation reservations with the applicant’s name.
- Insurance: Proof of travel insurance covering the entire stay in the Schengen area.
- For students: school registration.
- For employees: certificate of employment stating recruitment date, position, and salary.
- For self employed: company registration and tax card.
- Bank statement for the last 6 months. For minors, a parent’s bank statement.
- First time Applicants: Shahadat at Taharrukat translated into English.
- Language of Documents: All documents must be in English or Lithuanian, or accompanied by an official translation. Documents originally issued in English do not require translation.
- For Applicants Under 18:
- Original birth certificate translated into English.
- For Minors travelling alone: notarized certificate confirming the consent of both parents or legal guardian(s) to the minor’s travel. Copies of the parents’ or legal guardian(s)’ passports.
- For Minors travelling with one parent or legal guardian: notarized certificate confirming the consent of the non-accompanying parent or legal guardian to the minor’s travel. Copy of the parent or legal guardian’s passport . or legal guardian shall be signed in front of VFS Global agent,
- in case of one guardian is residing in another country, he/she should visit the nearest Lithuanian /Egyptian Embassy and sign the consent.
- Invitation letter:
- An invitation letter signed by host family/friends.
- The following Member States' consulates require that a specific form be used for the invitation letter: Austria, Finland, France, Germany, Hungary, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Slovenia, Slovakia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.

- Note: Movement certificate is not required for Visiting Family and Friends.
- If Lithuania is not the sole destination, the following conditions must be met:
- Applicant must spend more days in Lithuania than in any other Schengen country.
- Applicant spends an equal number of days in Lithuania and another country, Lithuania must be the first destination.
- If these conditions are not satisfied, the application will not be accepted.
- Applicants visiting multiple countries must provide a detailed travel itinerary.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in color and:
- Size of Photo is 5mm X 4.5mm
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling 90% of the photo is the head of applicant (without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa varies accordingly to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application is given. The regular visa processing time is 15 days upon completion of the application, the processing time may be extended to 30 or even 60 days calendar days, in cases that require a detailed assessment.
Applicant’s lack of travel plan will not fall on Embassy / Representative Office priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

## 

## Download Forms
For *online Visa Application *Form of the Republic of Lithuania which is designed to complete and submit the Application for a Schengen visa to the consular services of the Republic of Lithuania.
Technical specifications 
If you want to use Migris on your computer, here's what it takes:
- Microsoft Windows,
- Firefox 18.0, Internet Explorer IE 9, Google Chrome 24.0 or more recent versions,
- Adobe Acrobat Reader 10 (to download the Application Form),
- a printer (to print the Application Form).
To fill your application form kindly click here (https://www.migracija.lt/)
Checklists:
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a tourist visa.
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a business visa.
- Please
 
click here 
to download the checklist form for a sport visa.
- Please
 
click here  (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/lithuania/egypt/english/pdf/Lithuania-checklist-Visiting-family.pdf)
to download the checklist form for a visiting family visa.

visa_types.name: Sports Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Schengen Visa Application Requirements (Lithuania)
- Application Form (https://www.migracija.lt/)
: Completed Schengen visa application form through
- Photograph: One recent photo, glued to the form (not stapled).
- Travel Documents:
- Flight reservations showing the applicant’s name.
- Accommodation reservations with the applicant’s name.
- Insurance: Proof of travel insurance covering the entire stay in the Schengen area.
- For students: school registration.
- For employees: certificate of employment stating recruitment date, position, and salary.
- For self employed: company registration and tax card.
- Bank statement for the last 6 months. For minors, a parent’s bank statement.
- First time Applicants: Shahadat at Taharrukat translated into English.
- Language of Documents: All documents must be in English or Lithuanian, or accompanied by an official translation. Documents originally issued in English do not require translation.
- For Applicants Under 18:
- Original birth certificate translated into English.
- For Minors travelling alone: notarized certificate confirming the consent of both parents or legal guardian(s) to the minor’s travel. Copies of the parents’ or legal guardian(s)’ passports.
- For Minors travelling with one parent or legal guardian: notarized certificate confirming the consent of the non-accompanying parent or legal guardian to the minor’s travel. Copy of the parent or legal guardian’s passport . or legal guardian shall be signed in front of VFS Global agent,
- in case of one guardian is residing in another country, he/she should visit the nearest Lithuanian /Egyptian Embassy and sign the consent.
- Documents required for Sports:
- List of supporting documents to be submitted by applicants travelling for the purpose of cultural/sports events
- For Minors: Student card + original letter of the school mentioning: the full address, telephone number of the school, permission for absence, name and function of the person giving permission.
- Original letter of invitation from the organizer of the event in the Member State of destination
- If applicable: letter of the institution sending the concerned applicant.
- If Lithuania is not the sole destination, the following conditions must be met:
- Applicant must spend more days in Lithuania than in any other Schengen country.
- Applicant spends an equal number of days in Lithuania and another country, Lithuania must be the first destination.
- If these conditions are not satisfied, the application will not be accepted.
- Applicants visiting multiple countries must provide a detailed travel itinerary.
